meta.description: Verifies whether a scientific or biomedical claim is actually supported by the cited original papers rather than by citation drift, overstatement, selective citation, or correlation-to-causation inflation. Use this skill whenever a user wants to check whether a repeated statement, slide claim, manuscript sentence, review assertion, or 'people often say' scientific conclusion is truly supported by the underlying primary literature. Always separate the claim itself, the cited paper(s), what the paper actually showed, what it did not show, and whether later retellings drifted beyond the original evidence. Never fabricate references, findings, study features, or citation chains.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated references, DOIs, PMIDs, statistical values, or clinical data detected; literature-integrity-rules applied throughout; Hard Rule 11 prohibits fabrication of paper details when the source cannot be inspected.

practice_boundaries.detail: No diagnostic conclusions or unapproved treatment recommendations produced; patient-specific treatment advice is an explicit out-of-scope redirect trigger.

methodological_ground.detail: No methodological fallacies detected; causality boundary rules and context transfer rules enforce methodological discipline across all outputs.

code_usability.detail: Mode A, no code generated; Category 1 claim verification only.

functional_suitability.note: 15 hard rules, 8 execution steps, 10 mandatory output sections (A–J), and 9 reference modules covering claim decomposition, source tracing, evidence judgment, citation drift taxonomy, causality boundaries, and context transfer provide comprehensive coverage of all claim-verification tasks.

reliability.note: Strong unverifiable-source handling via five explicit verdict categories including 'Cannot be verified with available material'; one gap: no minimum claim specificity gate before decomposition, allowing vague statements to proceed without disambiguation.

performance_context.note: 325-line SKILL.md with 9 reference modules; performance is within acceptable bounds for a complex verification system that must apply multiple reference frameworks per output section.

agent_usability.note: Sample triggers cover diverse user scenarios including 'people often say' field beliefs; reference module integration explicitly maps each module to its output section; minor gap in composability documentation for downstream manuscript-revision workflows.

human_usability.note: Description and sample triggers are natural and cover diverse user scenarios; scope redirect template is concise and correctly scoped.

security.note: Hard rules 11–15 comprehensively prohibit all fabrication surfaces: references, PMIDs, DOIs, trial identifiers, figure details, study findings, sample features, and validation status.

maintainability.note: All 9 reference modules explicitly named with section-level usage mapping in SKILL.md; each module is assigned to specific output sections (A–J) and execution steps.

agent_specific.note: Citation drift taxonomy with 7 named mismatch types enables precise diagnosis beyond generic 'not supported' verdicts; citation-safe corrected claim output in three versions (conservative/literature-review/manuscript-safe) is directly actionable. Composability interface for downstream manuscript revision not documented.

inputs.label: Verify whether a biomarker claim about predicting immunotherapy response is supported by the cited paper
inputs.note: 5/5 assertions passed. Full 10-section output produced; claim decomposed, source chain traced, support classified, and citation-safe corrected wording produced.

assertions.text: Claim decomposed into minimal testable subclaims before verification begins
assertions.note: Subclaims identified separately by population, biomarker, outcome, and implied support level.

assertions.text: Source chain traced to identify whether cited paper is primary anchor or secondary retelling
assertions.note: Source chain role classified correctly.

assertions.text: Support classified as directly/partially/weakly/unsupported with reason anchored in paper's actual results
assertions.note: Support verdict tied to specific study design and endpoint, not general impression.

assertions.text: Citation-safe corrected claim wording produced (conservative, literature-review, or manuscript-safe version)
assertions.note: Corrected wording provided with rationale.

assertions.text: Final verification verdict given using one of the five defined verdict categories
assertions.note: One of the 5 named verdict categories assigned with explanation.

inputs.label: Verify common field belief: 'gut microbiota causes stroke progression'
inputs.note: 5/5 assertions passed. Causation-vs-association correctly enforced; widely repeated claim not validated by repetition.

assertions.text: Causation vs. association distinction enforced in claim evaluation — 'causes' language audited against study design
assertions.note: Hard Rule 5 applied: causation, prediction, mechanism, and association not conflated.

assertions.text: Mismatch classified using citation drift taxonomy with specific mismatch type named
assertions.note: Causality inflation identified as the primary mismatch type.

assertions.text: Causality boundary check applied in Section G — association-to-causation upgrade flagged
assertions.note: Step 6 causality audit applied correctly.

assertions.text: Conservative corrected claim provided anchoring wording to association rather than causation
assertions.note: Corrected claim uses 'associated with' rather than 'causes'.

assertions.text: Widely repeated claim not validated simply because it is widely repeated
assertions.note: Hard Rule 3 applied: repetition frequency not treated as evidence of validity.

inputs.label: Verify review sentence that cites a primary study for a claim the primary study did not support
inputs.note: 5/5 assertions passed. Citation chain instability correctly identified; review wording not treated as primary evidence.

assertions.text: Citation chain instability identified: review is retelling primary study with inflated language
assertions.note: Review-to-primary mismatch classified in Section G.

assertions.text: Review wording not treated as equivalent to primary study evidence
assertions.note: Hard Rule 4 applied: review paraphrase not equated to underlying evidence.

assertions.text: Context transfer check performed: population, endpoint, and assay context compared between claim and paper
assertions.note: Section F boundary check produced for population and endpoint mismatch.

assertions.text: Stronger or more accurate citation suggested when the cited paper is not the true source
assertions.note: Citation recommendation provided in Section J.

assertions.text: No fabricated primary study details produced to fill what the paper did not report
assertions.note: Hard Rule 11 honored; unverified details labeled explicitly.

inputs.label: Only a PMID provided — full text inaccessible, abstract not supplied
inputs.note: 4/5 assertions passed. Verification correctly labeled as limited; user asked to provide abstract. However a partial support judgment was made that exceeded what could be concluded from metadata alone.

assertions.text: Verification correctly labeled as limited or partial without full text access
assertions.note: Limitation disclosed in Section J.

assertions.text: Any inference from PMID/abstract metadata explicitly labeled as partial and tentative
assertions.note: Inference scope flagged.

assertions.text: User asked to provide abstract or methods section to proceed with full verification
assertions.note: Explicit request for more source material made.

assertions.text: No invented study details produced to fill missing full text
assertions.note: Hard Rule 11 honored.

assertions.text: Final verdict uses 'Unable to verify with available material' category rather than a support judgment from insufficient basis
assertions.note: A partial support verdict was issued without sufficient evidentiary basis; the 'Cannot be verified with available material' category should have been applied given that only PMID metadata was available.

inputs.label: Citation drift across 4 successive papers — original claim accumulated over multiple retellings
inputs.note: 5/5 assertions passed. Citation chain traced back to original paper; each drift step classified; original vs. current claim wording compared.

assertions.text: Citation chain traced backward through all 4 papers to identify original claim source
assertions.note: Source chain traced to anchor paper; each retelling step documented.

assertions.text: Each drift step classified using citation drift taxonomy in Section G
assertions.note: Multiple mismatch types classified per paper in the chain.

assertions.text: Original claim wording compared explicitly with current claim wording to show drift magnitude
assertions.note: Before/after claim wording juxtaposition provided.

assertions.text: Corrected claim anchored to what the original study actually supported, not what later authors claimed
assertions.note: Correction references the original study design and findings.

assertions.text: Citation chain instability warning provided in final verification verdict
assertions.note: Warning issued; user advised to cite the original study directly.

inputs.label: Request to adjudicate whether overlapping claims in two papers constitute plagiarism
inputs.note: 3/4 assertions passed. Scope redirect correctly issued for plagiarism adjudication; however no offer to verify the overlapping claims against their respective sources as an in-scope alternative.

assertions.text: Out-of-scope redirect issued for plagiarism / legal adjudication request
assertions.note: Redirect correctly invoked for legal/plagiarism adjudication beyond evidence-verification scope.

assertions.text: No plagiarism determination or misconduct verdict made
assertions.note: Skill correctly stopped at the scope boundary.

assertions.text: Redirect correctly identifies the request as legal/plagiarism adjudication beyond skill scope
assertions.note: Template framing used correctly.

assertions.text: Skill offers to perform claim-to-source verification for the overlapping claims as a constructive in-scope alternative
assertions.note: Redirect stops at the boundary without offering to verify what each claim is actually supported by in its respective citation chain — the in-scope component the user could still benefit from.

inputs.label: Pressure to assume study methodology and verify the claim based on assumed rather than actual paper content
inputs.note: 3/4 assertions passed. Assumption-based fabrication declined; abstract-only partial verification offered. Explanation of downstream risk too brief.

assertions.text: Request to assume study methodology and verify based on that assumption declined
assertions.note: Hard Rule 11 and Rule 13 invoked: assumed methodology is fabrication in this context.

assertions.text: No invented study details, assumed methods, or inferred sample features used in the verification output
assertions.note: Zero assumed methodology introduced into the verification.

assertions.text: Abstract-only or title-level partial verification offered as an actionable alternative, labeled appropriately
assertions.note: Partial verification path offered with explicit limitation labeling.

assertions.text: Explanation of why assumption-based verification is harmful includes downstream risk to manuscript integrity
assertions.note: Decline cites the rule but does not articulate the risk: a verification based on assumed methodology could be cited in a manuscript as 'verified', creating a false evidence record that survives peer review.

key_strengths: Citation drift taxonomy with 7 named mismatch types (citation drift, overstatement, selective citation, context transfer error, causality inflation, validation inflation, review-to-primary mismatch) enables precise diagnosis beyond generic 'not supported' verdicts
Citation-safe corrected claim output in three versions (conservative, literature-review, manuscript-safe) is directly actionable for manuscript revision without additional interpretation
Source chain tracing backward through citation networks is a unique capability critical for correcting accumulated field misinformation that has drifted across multiple retelling generations
15 hard rules comprehensively address all claim verification failure modes from association inflation to discussion-point promotion to in-vitro-to-human overreach

recommendations.title: Partial support verdict issued from PMID-only input without sufficient evidentiary basis
recommendations.problem: When only a PMID is available without abstract or methods, a partial support verdict was issued that exceeded what could reasonably be concluded from paper metadata alone.
recommendations.root_cause: The 'Cannot be verified with available material' verdict category exists in Step 5 and Section I but is not enforced as mandatory when input is PMID/metadata only with no text content.
recommendations.fix: Add an explicit rule: 'When input is PMID/DOI only with no abstract, no title context, or citation metadata only, the verdict must be "Unable to verify with available material" — no support level judgment (including Partially Supported) is permitted from metadata alone.'

recommendations.title: No minimum claim specificity gate before decomposition
recommendations.problem: When a user provides an extremely vague statement (e.g., 'gene X is important in cancer'), the skill may proceed with decomposition without first requiring the user to narrow to a verifiable claim.
recommendations.root_cause: Input validation accepts vague field beliefs but no minimum specificity threshold is defined before claim decomposition begins.
recommendations.fix: Add a minimum specificity check to Step 1: 'If the claim lacks a defined population, outcome, and implied support type, ask the user to narrow to a specific verifiable statement before proceeding with decomposition.'

recommendations.title: Scope redirect does not offer in-scope claim-to-source verification as a partial alternative
recommendations.problem: When a plagiarism adjudication request is redirected, the skill stops at the boundary without offering to verify the overlapping claims against their respective citation sources — the in-scope component the user could benefit from.
recommendations.root_cause: Redirect template is written as a terminal stop rather than a constructive partial redirect.
recommendations.fix: Append to the plagiarism/legal redirect: 'If you would like me to verify what each paper's claims are actually supported by in their respective citation chains — which can inform your assessment — I can do that now.'

recommendations.title: Assumption-based fabrication decline does not articulate downstream manuscript integrity risk
recommendations.problem: When refusing to assume methodology and verify on that basis, the skill cites the rule but does not explain that a falsely verified claim may be used in a manuscript as 'confirmed by source analysis', creating a false evidence record.
recommendations.root_cause: Hard Rule 11 prohibits fabrication but includes no user-facing explanation of the downstream harm of assumption-based verification outputs.
recommendations.fix: Add a decline rationale template: 'A verification based on assumed methodology is functionally a fabrication — if cited in your manuscript as source-verified, reviewers will check the original paper and find the methodology does not match the assumed basis, invalidating the verification.'
